pages.raw_text:
An interesting affair Of the week
will be the midwinter social to be
given tomorrow by the Spokane
County Pioneer society at the I'M
perial cafeteria in the Columbia
building. Two members, Mrs. Rob
ert Farley and Mrs. w. c. Grany,
have the arrangements in charge,
and most enjoyable musical and
social time is expected.


Members eligible to the society
must have resided in this city prior
to November, 1884.


z 4 4


The Masonic temple will be the
scene of an eventful affair tOmor-
row evening when the Scotch peo-
pie of the city will assemble to-
gether and celebrate the birthday
anniversary of ''Bobbie" Burns, the
great Scotch poet.


The social function is to be given
under the auspices of the st. An
drew society and plans are being
made to make the affair memoir
able one.


An elaborate dinner will be
served at $50 o'clock and will be
followed by a social evening, when
the guests will sing Scotch songs
and indulge in a general merry
making.


Mrs. David r. Mcclure and Mrs.
Conrad Bluhm have been among
the hostesses of the week who have
entertained at number of pretty
affairs. Tomorrow afternoon they
will entertain at the third of their
series of parties, when they will be
hostesses at thimble bee at the
Mcclure home on Augusta avenue.
About so guests have been included
on the invitation list.


The needIework department of
the Woman's club will meet tomor-
row morning at 8 a. m. at the home
OF the instructor. Mrs. w. w. Nc
Creary. 1924 Ninth avenue.


Mrs. A. p. Fassett will act as
chairman at the meeting of the par.
liamentary law class tomorrow at
las o'clock at the chamber of c6m-
merce.


The elocution department will
meet the same day at the chamber
or commerce. Mrs. Pauline Dunstan
Belden. teacher and Mrs. Jennie
Jones. chairman
